overview: This insect is the Target Longhorn Beetle (Hypsideroides junodi), a species of longhorn beetle (family Cerambycidae). It is known for its distinctive "bulls-eye" or concentric circle pattern on its elytra (wing covers), which helps it camouflage against wood. These beetles are found in southern Africa, particularly in regions like KwaZulu-Natal and the Western Cape. Like other cerambycids, they are wood-boring insects that feed on plant tissue. iNaturalist +2 3 sites Fit for a Tiny Robinhood | The Befuddled Loris
